steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Set up the angle sum equation using the isosceles condition
steps.content:
In any triangle, the three angles sum to 180°. Since ∠M = ∠N, let both equal $x$. Then:
$$x + x + 128^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$
$$2x + 128^{\circ} = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Solve for the measure of ∠M
steps.content:
Subtract 128° from both sides:
$$2x = 52^{\circ}$$
$$x = 26^{\circ}$$
Since ∠M = $x$, the measure of ∠M is 26°. That matches Choice A — 26°.
